assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Repository Registry' section with a table listing both 'trustify-backend' (serena_backend) and 'trustify-ui' (serena_ui) as entries
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 20: '## Repository Registry', line 24: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |', line 25: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |'. Both repositories are listed with their correct Serena instance names.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Jira Configuration' section with all 5 fields: Project key (TC), Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID (10142), Git Pull Request custom field (customfield_10875), GitHub Issue custom field (customfield_10747)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 27: '## Jira Configuration', line 29: 'Project key: TC', line 30: 'Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432', line 31: 'Feature issue type ID: 10142', line 32: 'Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875', line 33: 'GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747'. All 5 fields present with correct values.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Code Intelligence' section that documents the mcp__<instance>__<tool> naming convention
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 35: '## Code Intelligence', line 37: 'Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.' The naming convention is explicitly documented.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '### Limitations' subheading under Code Intelligence
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 47: '### Limitations' appears under the '## Code Intelligence' section (line 35). It is a subheading within Code Intelligence as required.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md mentions discovering 2 Serena instances (serena_backend and serena_ui) from the MCP tool listing
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 2, line 10: 'Source: MCP tool listing (`mcp-tools-with-serena.md`)', lines 13-15: 'Discovered instances: `serena_backend` — identified from tools `mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol`...' and '`serena_ui` — identified from tools `mcp__serena_ui__find_symbol`...'. Both instances discovered from MCP tool listing.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates all Project Configuration sections (Repository Registry, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, Bug Configuration) were newly added — pre-existing non-configuration content from the CLAUDE.md (like the project heading and Documentation section) may appear as preserved, which is expected since only Project Configuration was missing
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md: Preserved section (lines 3-7) lists project title, Documentation, Getting Started as unchanged. Line 11: 'The entire Project Configuration section was added since none existed in the original CLAUDE.md.' All four sections marked as '(new)': '## Repository Registry (new)' (line 13), '## Jira Configuration (new)' (line 23), '## Code Intelligence (new)' (line 33), '## Bug Configuration (new)' (line 41).

assertion_results.text: The Repository Registry table contains columns: Repository, Role, Serena Instance, Path — matching project-config.template.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 22: '| Repository | Role | Serena Instance | Path |'. All four required columns are present in the correct order.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md contains a '## Bug Configuration' section with three fields: Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 52: '## Bug Configuration', line 54: 'Bug issue type ID: 10001', line 55: 'Bug template: [docs/bug-template.md](docs/bug-template.md)', line 56: 'Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks'. All three fields present with correct values.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers to enable security triage for the project (Step 9 opt-in prompt) and respects the user's decline — outputs/claude-md-result.md does NOT contain a '## Security Configuration' section
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 63-65: 'Step 10 — Security Configuration: User declined to enable security triage for this project. Section not created.' changes-log.md lines 59-60: '## Security Configuration' listed under 'Not Added — user declined to enable security triage for this project.' claude-md-result.md does NOT contain any '## Security Configuration' heading. The opt-in was offered and the decline was respected.
